hero.question: Will The Greens win the most seats in the 2026 Baden-Württemberg parliamentary elections?
hero.prob_take: The Greens just jumped 26 points in 24 hours, signaling real money believes they're outrunning traditional left-right dynamics in Germany's richest state. At 40%, you're getting odds that ignore their polling leads and the structural advantage of German voters rewarding anti-establishment parties in regional elections where turnout is low and protest votes stick.

daily_take.headline: German Greens Surge 26 Points in One Day. Are Bettors Panicking or Seeing Something Real?
daily_take.deck: The Greens jumped from 14% to 40% odds on Baden-Württemberg's 2026 election in 24 hours on $269K volume, while CDU odds cratered. This is either a massive overreaction to a single poll or smart money front-running a real shift in regional politics. The play: dig into what triggered this move before the market corrects or confirms it. CDU at 59% still looks like the safer fade if you believe in reversion, but volume this heavy usually means someone knows something.

sidebar.headline: CDU's 27-point collapse in one day screams correction waiting to happen. Either the Greens poll showed something shocking or this is panic selling that'll reverse hard.
sidebar.label: CDU wins most seats: 59.4%

sidebar.headline: Japan favored at 90% in pool play feels right until you check the actual matchup. Value hunters should monitor line movement here.
sidebar.label: Chinese Taipei beats Japan: 10.1%

sidebar.headline: Iran closing the Strait sits at 31.5% with almost zero volume. Could be massively under or overpriced depending on geopolitical read, but the illiquidity makes it a trap for retail.
sidebar.label: Iran closes Strait through Aug 2026: 31.5%
